message:
🐛 Fix 13 CNCF install missions with incorrect configs (#1376)

- chaos-mesh: Remove fabricated RBAC step, fix dashboard port (2333:2333),
  fix uninstall CRD cleanup to use label selector
- falco: Fix helm repo name (falcosecurity), chart name (falcosecurity/falco),
  namespace (falco), remove kubectl edit deployment (DaemonSet), remove
  fabricated port-forward step
- flagger: Add service mesh prerequisite note, namespace guidance
- headlamp: Change namespace from headlamp to kube-system (official)
- inspektor-gadget: Change namespace to gadget, remove kubectl edit deployment
  (DaemonSet), remove fabricated dashboard/port-forward step
- k8sgpt: Fix chart name (k8sgpt-operator), namespace (k8sgpt-operator-system),
  remove unnecessary image.repository/tag flags
- keda: Remove fabricated RBAC step (Helm handles RBAC)
- kubeslice: Fix container image from botkube to kubeslice-controller,
  fix sourceUrls from botkube to kubeslice
- kuma: Change namespace from kuma to kuma-system, update maturity to graduated
- litmus: Fix helm repo name (litmuschaos), chart ref (litmuschaos/litmus)
- openebs: Fix helm repo URL, remove fabricated RBAC step, fix uninstall
  CRD cleanup (replace --all with label selector)
- opentelemetry-operator: Add cert-manager prerequisite note with workaround
- strimzi: Add OCI-based install note as preferred method

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:

```"
+        "description": "Add the official Falco Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash

```"

```"

    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"

    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "250m"
```"

```"

       {
         "title": "Update repo and run upgrade command",
-        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade Falco to the latest version using the following commands:
```bash

```"
+        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade Falco to the latest version using the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade falco falcosecurity/falco --namespace falco --version 0.44.0
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check that the Falco pod is running the new version successfully:
```bash

```"
+        "description": "Check that the Falco pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco
```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
         "title": "Falco pod is not starting",
-        "description": "If the Falco pod is in a 'CrashLoopBackOff' state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n falco-system deployment/falco
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing permissions."
+        "description": "If the Falco pod is in a 'CrashLoopBackOff' state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing permissions."
       },
       {
         "title": "Falco alerts not triggering",
-        "description": "If Falco is not generating alerts, ensure that the rules are correctly configured. Check the rules file:
```bash
kubectl exec -n falco-system -it $(kubectl get pods -n falco-system -l app=falco -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- cat /etc/falco/falco_rules.yaml
``` 
Make sure the rules are enabled and correctly defined."
+        "description": "If Falco is not generating alerts, ensure that the rules are correctly configured. Check the rules file:
```bash
kubectl exec -n falco -it $(kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- cat /etc/falco/falco_rules.yaml
``` 
Make sure the rules are enabled and correctly defined."
       },
       {
-        "title": "Port forwarding not working",
-        "description": "If you cannot access Falco's output via port forwarding, ensure that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n falco-system
``` 
If the service is not available, check the deployment status and logs."
+        "title": "Falco events not appearing",
+        "description": "If Falco events are not appearing, ensure the DaemonSet is running on all nodes:
```bash
kubectl get ds -n falco
kubectl get svc -n falco
``` 
If pods are not running, check the DaemonSet status and logs."
       },
       {
         "title": "Resource limits causing OOMKilled",
-        "description": "If the Falco pod is being killed due to out-of-memory (OOM) issues, consider increasing the memory limits in the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment falco -n falco-system
``` 
Adjust the 'resources.limits.memory' value accordingly."
+        "description": "If the Falco pod is being killed due to out-of-memory (OOM) issues, consider increasing the memory limits via Helm values:
```bash
helm upgrade falco falcosecurity/falco --namespace falco --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi
``` 
Adjust the memory limit value accordingly."
       }
     ]
   },

     "title": "Install and Configure Strimzi on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "Strimzi provides a way to run an Apache Kafka® cluster on Kubernetes or OpenShift in various deployment configurations. Installing Strimzi allows you to easily manage Kafka clusters and their components within your Kubernetes environment.",
+    "description": "Strimzi provides a way to run an Apache Kafka cluster on Kubernetes or OpenShift in various deployment configurations. Installing Strimzi allows you to easily manage Kafka clusters and their components within your Kubernetes environment.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
         "title": "Add the Strimzi Helm repository",
-        "description": "Add the Strimzi Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Strimzi charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts\nhelm repo update\n```"
+        "description": "Add the Strimzi Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Strimzi charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts\nhelm repo update\n```\n\n**Note:** OCI-based install is now the preferred method. You can alternatively install directly via OCI:\n```bash\nhelm install strimzi oci://quay.io/strimzi-helm/strimzi-kafka-operator -n strimzi --create-namespace\n```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Install Strimzi Kafka Operator",
